arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. FC Bayern München - Wed, Oct 2nd 2024, 20:00 The UEFA Champions League anthem will blast out at Villa Park on Wednesday night when Aston Villa host Bayern Munich. Unai Emery's team began the league phase with a convincing victory against BSC Young Boys and will now attempt to take down the Bundesliga side. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit to Villa Park on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Click here for more information. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets General Admission tickets are sold out and only a limited number of hospitality and GA+ seats remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. More information can be found here. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information here and book online. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3’s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 6.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Aston Villa supporters can pre-book official matchday parking for the first time thanks to new partnerships. Supporters can pre-book their space via either of the links below. www.astonvillaparking.co.uk www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk Supporters are also advised of the following matchday parking sites around Villa Park: Powerleague (£10 – payment by cash or card) Aston Tower School (£7 - cash only) Birchfield School (£7 - cash only) Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Please plan ahead and leave more time for your journey. To plan your journey by public transport, visit www.tfwm.org.uk. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Accessibility signage installed at Witton Station Accessibility signage was fitted at Witton Station last season thanks in large part to an Aston Villa supporter. Cameron Wood is a wheelchair user and disability rights campaigner who travels to and from games at Villa Park via train. Cameron wrote to the club to voice his concerns about accessibility at the station on a matchday and a meeting was subsequently arranged with West Midlands Trains. Following the meeting, new accessibility signage, which can be seen from both ends of the platform by station and onboard staff, was fitted. Find out more here. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on Wednesday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 5pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area, food and big screen TVs. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 6.30pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Quadrox and a Q&A with former players. Family Fun Zone Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 5pm. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. The Warehouse Design Update The Warehouse, which will now be 40,000 sqft, has been further developed with an enhanced design to accommodate live music and entertainment events for 3,500+ visitors and fans. Structural alterations to over half the rooftop will make way for a newly raised flat roof which will accommodate a generous mezzanine floor and viewing galley from above for both live music and fan zone entertainment. Creating a multi-use space called ‘The Warehouse’, will include a diverse range of Food & Beverage offers and pre- and post-match and event entertainment. The Warehouse will become a vibrant and ‘go to’ destination for the community embedded in the Villa Park and Witton Street scene. Matchday Programme Aston Villa supporters can secure a piece of history by ordering the official matchday programme from the club's first home game in the UEFA Champions League. The programme for Wednesday night's match against Bayern Munich features a special silver foil design on the cover and is packed with interviews and features. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9am-8pm and 9.30-10.30pm Holte Store: 5-8pm and 9.30-10.30pm 82 Lounge: 5-8pm and 8.30-10.30pm Lower Grounds: 5-8pm and 8.30-10.30pm Trinity: 6.30pm until the end of half-time During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards collection point will be open from 5.30-7.30pm. The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here.

arts.ttl: Get Matchday Ready: Bayern Munich at Villa Park
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. FC Bayern München - Wed, Oct 2nd 2024, 20:00 Aston Villa host their first Champions League fixture at Villa Park on Wednesday night as Bayern Munich are the visitors to B6. Ahead of the eagerly-awaited clash, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple / Google Wallet for NFC entry. Tickets null Have your ticket ready For swift entry, have your Bayern Munich ticket ready before you reach the turnstile. Please confirm that your ticket corresponds to the Bayern Munich match and not a previous fixture. Do not take screenshots. Avoid reliance on the stadium's internet connectivity for e-ticket access. Ensure your ticket is available for use at the turnstile before you arrive at the stadium when you have a strong internet connection. Do not take screenshots. If you have asked for duplicate tickets, please remember that previous copies will not work, and only the most recent one will grant entry. Please present your ticket in one of two ways Aston Villa App (internet connection required) Open the app to the Tickets page before you get to the stadium. Keep the app running in the background for easy switching when in the queue for the turnstile. For in-app tickets with a barcode, scan the code at the bottom of the reader. Do not attempt to take screenshots of the QR code, these will not work due to rotating barcodes being in place for security reasons. Apple or Google Wallet Add your ticket to your Apple or Google Wallet for a seamless "tap and go" experience. Do this by clicking the link in your ticket confirmation email and selecting 'add to wallet'. For tickets downloaded to your Apple or Google wallet, tap phone on the top of the reader. All turnstiles at Villa Park have now been upgraded, you will be greeted by the below screen when you get to the turnstile. If you are using a ticket in your wallet then tap your phone at the top. If you are using an in-app barcode then scan at the bottom of the reader.

arts.ttl: Paul Lambert hits out at bizarre Harry Kane ‘low blow’ before Aston Villa Bayern Munich clash
arts.sum: Former Aston Villa manager Paul Lambert has launched a defence of Harry Kane ahead of the Champions League clash with Bayern Munich at Villa Park. In a statement on Kane’s injury, Bayern said: "Harry Kane suffered a painful blow to his ankle in Saturday's top Bundesliga match against Bayer Leverkusen 1-1.

arts.ttl: When is Aston Villa - Bayern Munich? how to watch on TV, stream online
arts.sum: The game between Aston Villa and Bayern Munich will be played on Wednesday October 2, with kick off at 3:00 p.m. ET / 12:00 p.m. PT. Form guide for Aston Villa - Bayern Munich

arts.ttl: Breaking: Bayern Munich hires new head of scouting
arts.sum: As expected, Bayern Munich has hired Nils Schmadtke to lead its scouting department after the departure of Markus Pilawa. In the summer of 2020, he became head of the scouting department at VfL Wolfsburg before returning to Borussia Mönchengladbach as sporting director after two seasons.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Sven Ulreich calls Simon Rolfes a “wan**r” during 1-1 draw with Bayer Leverkusen, has caught the attention of the DFB
arts.sum: Of course, this incident somewhat mirrors Leverkusen CEO Fernando Carro’s derogatory remarks about Bayern board member for sport Max Eberl and the subsequent reaction from Rolfes was not much different from the one Bayern had, as captured again by @iMiaSanMia : “I neither heard nor noticed it. There seems to be a video circulating of Sven Ulreich insulting Leverkusen sporting director Simon Rolfes by saying ‘Sit down Rolfes, you wan**r’.

arts.ttl: Mortimer: Villa have every chance against Bayern
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. FC Bayern München - Wed, Oct 2nd 2024, 20:00 Dennis Mortimer believes Aston Villa “have got every chance” when they face Bayern Munich on Wednesday night – just like his European Cup-winning side of 1982. Villa’s captain from that memorable night in Rotterdam sat down with The Official Aston Villa Podcast ahead of our UEFA Champions League encounter. LISTEN NOW Peter Withe’s 67th-minute strike proved the difference in the final 42 years ago, with Mortimer lifting the trophy to the sky to mark the club’s most famous night. Speaking to The Official Aston Villa Podcast, Mortimer recalls his younger days, joining Villa, making his debut, his career in claret and blue, becoming captain and securing silverware. The midfielder, among other glorious moments, reflects on our First Division title-winning campaign and the club’s 1982 European Cup triumph, including the final against Bayern. Wednesday’s clash at Villa Park will be only the second-ever meeting between the two clubs. And Mortimer said: “I think they (the current squad) have got every chance, like we did. “You can’t go into a game thinking ‘it’s Bayern Munich’. They’re a great team, they’ve got great tradition, the number of times they’ve been in the semi-finals, number of times they’ve won it. “They’re one of the great clubs of Europe. “But, what I find about Aston Villa at the moment, is they had a great season last season, they were phenomenal in the way they played. “That’s all down to the manager because he brought a system into that team which has really complemented the players themselves and their attributes. “That system is there because he’s identified the players, he’s put them in there and they’ve worked it for him. “They know exactly what they need to do, exactly like we did; we had a team and we knew what we had to do. “I think what Unai Emery has done, he’s given them a real focus on the football pitch on how to play the game of football.” The Official Aston Villa Podcast is here! Welcome to our latest new regular offering for all supporters, available for free on all your favourite podcast platforms and also in video format on VillaTV+. Co-hosted by fans’ favourite Alan McInally and VillaTV presenter Kate Tracey, The Official Aston Villa Podcast brings you all things claret and blue. Whether you're on the move, in the gym or at home, simply search 'Official Villa Podcast' on your podcast app or watch on www.avfc.co.uk/video. Apple, click here Spotify, click here Podfollow, click here

arts.ttl: Aston Villa told key "difference" could sink Bayern Munich in Champions League
arts.sum: Former Aston Villa centre-back Richard Dunne tells Sports Mole the one key difference that could help the Lions shock Aston Villa in the Champions League.

arts.ttl: Netherlands legend Wesley Sneijder fires Aston Villa barb at Man United amid Erik ten Hag scrutiny
arts.sum: Wesley Sneijder mocked Manchester United's alleged strategy for manager Erik ten Hag amid the upcoming big games against Porto and Aston Villa . In conversation with Rondo, via the Manchester Evening News, Sneijder dismissed the murmurs, saying: "We now read reports, 'This is the week of truth for Erik ten Hag'.

arts.ttl: Kane returns to training for Bayern ahead of Aston Villa clash
arts.sum: Harry Kane returned to training on Tuesday afternoon after picking up a minor injury in Bayern's 1-1 draw with Bayer Leverkusen. This means he should be fit to start against Aston Villa on Wednesday.
